ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anoint: verb. To smear or rub over with oil or an unctuous substance; also, to spread over, as oil.
ant: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Antakarinya.
att: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pamplona Atta.
inn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isinai.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iota: noun. The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet (Ι, ι).
nan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Min Nan.
nation: noun. A historically constituted, stable community of people, formed based on a common language, territory, economic life, ethnicity and/or
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
noon: noun. The time of day when the Sun seems to reach its highest point in the sky; solar noon.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
notation: noun. The act, process, method, or an instance of representing by a system or set of marks, signs, figures, or characters.
notion: noun. Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.
oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
onion: noun. A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
taint: noun. A contamination, decay or putrefaction, especially in food.
tanto: noun. A traditional Japanese small sword or knife; often used as a secondary weapon to a katana.
tao: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yami.
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titan: noun. Something or someone of very large stature, greatness, or godliness.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
toot: noun. The noise of a horn or whistle.
tot: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Patla-Chicontla Totonac.
naio: noun. Myoporum sandwicense, a flowering plant in the figwort family.
nato: noun. A tree of the genus Mora
nina: noun. A hidden message revealed in the completed grid of a crossword.
nitta: noun. A tropical tree of the genus Parkia, having edible pods and seeds, especially Parkia filicoidea or Parkia biglobosa.
noa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Woun Meu.
non: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old Norse.
oni: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Onin.
oon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Önge.
otiant: adjective. At ease, at leisure; idling, indolent.
oto: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Oto-Pamean languages.
tai: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Tai languages.
tinta: noun. A kind of wine of a deep red color, chiefly from Galicia or Malaga in Spain.
tnt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tontemboan.
toa: noun. A small painted artifact made by the Diyari people of Australia, believed to have been used as place markers or signposts.
tonation: noun. The tonal properties of a sound.
tonto: noun. Synonym of idiot: someone who is foolish or stupid.
toona: noun. A plant of the genus Toona; red cedar.
toto: noun. Only used in in toto
